transcript.clean_text: wide open planes with a sting in the tail on stage 16 of lovuelta well before the rest day it was this man aaronsmann delighted to have taken a stage with likewise his dsm team a glorious leader still remcor evan apoll albeit by a reduced margin after ranging attacks from primos roglic over the previous two days how would today shape up well it was one of those where a breakaway was expected to dominate and it almost felt like an extension to a rest day for much of the peloton hello party atmosphere extended to the riders themselves they're in a good mood as well they might be when co-avenue pop leading this race and the points jersey firmly upon the shoulders of matt's past so what lay ahead well on an apparently flat day hid a sting in the tail of course shaped like a head coming over the back and indeed finishing with some very testy ramps after the intermediate sprint ramps of 8.3 percent extending over 600 meters lay ahead also a very long day in the saddle particularly for the breakaway just two up front okamika of burgos bh and luis angel mate in the orange of oscar tell us he pledging to plant a tree for every kilometer he was in the break and today that was a lot of trees shooting the breeze much of the pelican to be honest they knowing that two riders up the road was no threat and that this ultimately would surely be one for the sprinters we would see the intermediate sprint itself julie came and went and was sufficiently fought for with mate taking ahead of okamika the remaining points hovered up by who else other than mads pedersen well that sting in the tail was to turn this into a hard man's day be that from the gc or indeed from those sprinters who could take an incline at the end and boy oh boy did we have some drama the pace picked up the breakaway absorbed and first to falter kaden groves with a puncture at the end of his ambitions plenty being shown by primos roglic he instigated the move of the afternoon straight onto his wheel well pascal ackermann was being guided he now turning himself into a ramp finisher it seemed roglic went ackermann on his tail and of course mass pedestal not that far behind in the green jersey fred wright was also coming to join the fun no fun at all for this man the leader remko evanapol puncturing within the last three kilometers his fortune of fortune favors the brave and sometimes it hampers the brave as well five came to the line including danny van popple and all the aforementioned riders primos roglic then just had to tuck in for his advantage but the rising knee of fred wright caught his bars and down he went roglic on the deck would be credited with the same time as those he was sharing the sprint with pedersen took it but roglich battered and bruised picked himself back up off the asphalt and crawled across the line well at what cost later in this race we asked ourselves roglic looking very second hand evan apoll with that fortunate puncture late on across the line to be credited with the same time as the main body of the pelican the gap between the pair of them then modest changes overall but what changes to primos roglic for the next few days in the run to madrid our hope is that he recovers well congratulations to matt pederson for taking his second stage win that green jersey all but belonging to him what a dramatic finale and what a change overall is this the end of the roglich challenge we hope not evan nepal looked fresh and untroubled by his own problems late on he leads the race albeit by a reduced margin but not by much a minute and 26 is the gap as we continue our run towards madrid so what of stage seventeen tentutia awaiting a looping course a monastery at the end upon amount a category two test at the end and a day that looks certain to garner a big breakaway with ambition the road to madrid is a rough one you
